data.title: InMode Shares Are Rallying After Hours – What’s The Takeover Offer?
data.description:
Key points:
  • Steel Partners has offered $18.00 per share to acquire a majority stake in InMode. 
  • The investment firm said the proposal would be fully funded from cash on hand and borrowing capacity under its existing credit facility, with no financing contingency. 
  • The firm may elect to proceed with bringing a binding proposal directly to the shareholders, in case the management and board of InMode did not engage with it.

Inmode shares rallied during extended hours of trading on Wednesday after investment firm Steel Partners presented an offer to buy a majority of the company. 

Shares rallied more than 8% at the time of writing. 

Details Of The Offer

Steel Partners said it is pursuing an acquisition of 51% of InMode’s outstanding shares for $18.00 per share. It said the offer represented a 29% premium over the company’s closing share price on January 23, 2026, before speculation around a potential transaction was reported in the media.

“We have also recently attempted to engage in a private dialogue with InMode’s leadership around our attractive proposal, but have so far been rebuffed. Therefore, we believe it is imperative that you, the company’s true owners, are made aware of this opportunity,” it said.

Steel Partners said the proposal would be fully funded from cash on hand and borrowing capacity under its existing credit facility, with no financing contingency. 

Steel Partners may elect to proceed with bringing a binding proposal directly to the shareholders, in case the management and board of InMode does not engage with it. 

Critical Of Returns

Steel Partners said that InMode has underperformed its true potential, with ‘abysmal’ total shareholder return (TSR) over numerous periods. 

“It is concerning that, despite enjoying the enviable position of having a large amount of cash on its balance sheet and no debt, InMode has not taken steps to consistently return its outsized amount of cash on hand to shareholders in an accretive manner,” it added. 

How Did Stocktwits Users React?

Retail sentiment around INMD trended in ‘extremely bullish’ amid ‘extremely high’ message volume. 

Shares in the company have fallen nearly 12% over the past 12 months.

data.title: Investors cheer the end of Southwest's open-seating era, as airline forecasts a big 2026 profit
data.description:
By Bill Peters

Southwest stock jumps in extended trading after airline forecasts a larger-than-expected profit this year

Southwest has faced pressure from activist investors to improve its bottom line.

On Tuesday, Southwest Airlines' five-decade-long open-seating policy came to an end. On Wednesday, investors appeared thrilled to enter the airline's first year without it.

Shares of Southwest (LUV) jumped 6.6% after hours on Wednesday, after the airline forecast a profit this year that came in well above Wall Street's expectations, helped by a move to assigned seating that took place a day ago and a wider variety of upsells for flights.

Executives said that 2026 was off to a solid start, barring the impact of Winter Storm Fern, which has led to thousands of flight cancellations nationwide. Southwest's earnings call with analysts takes place on Thursday morning.

The carrier said it expects adjusted earnings per share of at least $4 this year, with a 2% to 3% increase in its flight coverage. That would mark a 300% gain over last year.

Analysts polled by FactSet were expecting a per-share profit of $3.22 for 2026. If Southwest hits that $4 target, it would mark the biggest per-share profit since 2019, before the pandemic upended the airline industry.

Southwest's first-quarter adjusted earnings-per-share forecast was also above expectations. The company forecast at least 45 cents a share on that front, above Wall Street's estimates for 30 cents. Southwest said those forecasts included the winter storm's impact.

The company also forecast a minimum 9.5% jump in unit revenue, a much-watched industry metric that measures sales in relation to flight capacity.

Along with the move away from open seating, Southwest over the past year has introduced more fees, basic economy fares and seats with extra legroom. The company had faced pressure from activist investors to improve its bottom line. Rival carriers such as Delta Air Lines Inc. (DAL) and United Airlines Holdings Inc. (UAL) have taken steps over the years to attract wealthier travelers and break up their flight cabins into different seating classes.

For its fourth quarter, Southwest reported adjusted earnings per share of 58 cents, topping estimates. Revenue of $7.44 billion missed estimates for $7.5 billion.

-Bill Peters

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Sprock-it Acquisitions and U92 Announce TSXV Conditional Acceptance, Filing of Filing Statement and Filing of Technical Report
data.description:
Toronto, Ontario--(Newsfile Corp. - January 28, 2026) - Sprock-it Acquisitions Ltd. (TSXV: SPRO.P) ("Sprock-it" or the "Company"), a capital pool company, and U92 Corp. ("U92", and together with the Company, the "Parties") are pleased to announce that the Parties have received conditional acceptance from the TSX Venture Exchange ("TSXV") for the closing of the previously-announced arm's length qualifying transaction of the Parties (the "Qualifying Transaction"). In connection with the Qualifying Transaction, the Parties have publicly filed a filing statement dated January 28, 2026 (the "Filing Statement"), prepared in accordance with the requirements of the TSXV, and a technical report in respect of the Kurupung Project with an effective date of July 30, 2025 (the "Technical Report"), prepared in accordance with National Instrument 43-101 - Standards for Disclosure for Mineral Projects. The Filing Statement and the Technical Report are both available under the Company's issuer profile on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca.

Further to the Parties' comprehensive news release dated September 9, 2025, the Qualifying Transaction will be completed by way of a three-cornered amalgamation (the "Amalgamation") under the Business Corporations Act (Ontario) among the Company, U92 and a wholly-owned subsidiary of the Company incorporated for the purpose of completing the Amalgamation. The Amalgamation will result in the reverse takeover of the Company by the shareholders of U92, following which, the Company, as the issuer resulting therefrom (the "Resulting Issuer"), is expected to carry on the current business of U92 under the name "U92 Energy Corp." or such other name as may be determined by U92 and deemed acceptable to the applicable regulatory authorities. It is anticipated that the common shares of the Resulting Issuer will trade on the TSXV under the ticker symbol "UTWO" shortly following the closing of the Qualifying Transaction.

The completion of the Qualifying Transaction is subject to a number of conditions including, but not limited to, receipt of all required regulatory approvals, including final TSXV acceptance, and satisfaction of other customary closing conditions. Assuming all conditions for closing are satisfied, the closing of the Qualifying Transaction is expected to occur on or about January 29, 2026, or such other date as the Parties may determine.

For further information regarding the Qualifying Transaction, please see the Filing Statement, which is available under the Company's issuer profile on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca.

About U92 Corp.

U92 was incorporated under the Business Corporations Act (Ontario) on December 13, 2024. U92 is not a reporting issuer in any province or territory of Canada.

U92 is a uranium exploration company, which, through its wholly owned subsidiaries, indirectly owns the exploration rights for the Kurupung Project in Guyana, South America. U92 intends to focus on advancing the Kurupung Project. U92 is targeting sustainable, high-grade uranium resources to support the global clean energy transition and is strategically positioned to benefit from the growing demand and supply deficit in the uranium market. U92's flagship Kurupung Project holds a 92.2 km² land package in the Aricheng Batholith, a well-established shear-hosted uranium district in Guyana.

The Technical Report in respect of the flagship Kurupung Project has been filed with the appropriate Canadian securities regulatory authorities. The Technical Report was prepared in accordance with NI 43-101 and is available for review under the Company's SEDAR+ profile at www.sedarplus.ca.

About Sprock-it Acquisitions Ltd.

Sprock-it is a capital pool company, incorporated for the purposes of identifying and evaluating businesses or assets with a view to completing a Qualifying Transaction (as such term is defined under the policies of the TSXV). Sprock-it was incorporated on July 29, 2024 under the Business Corporations Act (Alberta) and is a reporting issuer in the provinces of Alberta, British Columbia and Ontario. The common shares of Sprock-it (the "Sprock-it Shares") are listed for trading on the TSXV. The Sprock-it Shares were halted on June 26, 2025 in connection with the announcement of the Transaction and remain halted as at the date hereof. Until completion of a Qualifying Transaction, Sprock-it will not carry on any business other than the identification and evaluation of assets or businesses with a view to completing a proposed Qualifying Transaction.

Additional Information

All information contained in this news release with respect to U92 and Sprock-it was supplied by the respective parties for inclusion herein, without independent review by the other party, and each party and its directors and officers have relied on the other party for any information concerning the other party.

For further information regarding Sprock-it, U92, the Resulting Issuer or the Transaction, please refer to the filing statement in connection with the Transaction which is available under Sprock-it's SEDAR+ profile at www.sedarplus.ca.

For more information, please contact:

Jeff Paquin, Chief Executive Officer

Email: jpaquin@spokeresources.com

Phone: (+1) 403 970-7343

Adam Clode, Chief Executive Officer

info@u92corp.com

Cautionary Note Regarding Forward-Looking Information

This news release contains forward-looking statements and forward-looking information (collectively, "forward-looking statements") within the meaning of applicable securities laws. Any statements that are contained in this news release that are not statements of historical fact may be deemed to be forward-looking statements. Forward-looking statements are often identified by terms such as "may", "should", "anticipate", "will", "estimates", "believes", "intends", "expects" and similar expressions which are intended to identify forward-looking statements. More particularly and without limitation, this news release contains forward-looking statements concerning the Qualifying Transaction (including the anticipated timing of the closing thereof), and the Resulting Issuer (including, the anticipated business focus of the Resulting Issuer, as well as the ticker symbol under which the common shares of the Resulting Issuer are expected to trade on the TSXV). Forward-looking statements are inherently uncertain, and the actual performance may be affected by a number of material factors, assumptions and expectations, many of which are beyond the control of the Parties, including expectations and assumptions concerning (i) the Company, U92, the Resulting Issuer and the Qualifying Transaction, (ii) the timely receipt of all required shareholder and regulatory approvals (as applicable), including the final approval of the TSXV, and (iii) the satisfaction of other closing conditions in accordance with the terms of the business combination agreement previously entered into by the Parties. Readers are cautioned that assumptions used in the preparation of any forward-looking statements may prove to be incorrect. Events or circumstances may cause actual results to differ materially from those predicted as a result of numerous known and unknown risks, uncertainties and other factors, many of which are beyond the control of the Parties. Readers are further cautioned not to place undue reliance on any forward-looking statements, as such information, although considered reasonable by the respective management of the Parties at the time of preparation, may prove to be incorrect and actual results may differ materially from those anticipated.

The forward-looking statements contained in this news release are made as of the date of this news release, and are expressly qualified by the foregoing cautionary statement. Except as expressly required by securities law, neither Party undertakes any obligation to update publicly or to revise any of the included forward-looking statements, whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise.

Completion of the Qualifying Transaction is subject to a number of conditions. There can be no assurance that the Qualifying Transaction will be completed at all.

Investors are cautioned that, except as disclosed in the Filing Statement, any information released or received with respect to the Qualifying Transaction may not be accurate or complete and should not be relied upon. Trading in the securities of a capital pool company should be considered highly speculative.

The TSXV has in no way passed upon the merits of the Qualifying Transaction and has neither approved nor disapproved the contents of this news release. This news release shall not constitute an offer to sell or the solicitation of an offer to buy any securities in any jurisdiction.

Neither the TSXV nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in the policies of the TSXV) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

Not for distribution to United States newswire services or for release, publication, distribution or

dissemination, directly or indirectly, in whole or in part, in or into the United States.

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/282004

data.title: Nikkei May Rise as Weaker Yen Supports Earnings Hopes — Market Talk
data.description:
1849 ET - Japanese stocks may rise as a weaker yen supports hopes for stronger corporate earnings. Nikkei futures are up 0.3% at 53845 on the SGX. USD/JPY is at 153.06, up from 152.62 as of Wednesday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are focusing on earnings as well as Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's economic plans ahead of the general election in early February. Takeda Pharmaceutical, Fujitsu and Canon are set to report their results later in the day. The Nikkei Stock Average closed flat at 53358.71 on Wednesday. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com)

1740 ET - The AUD/USD has cracked 0.70 for the first time in three years, prompting UBS to assess what that could mean for Australian stocks. It finds that the Australian equity market typically has a 'pro-cyclical' relationship to AUD moves. Mining stocks usually lead the way. "This 'amplifying' relationship is less to do with earnings translations, and seems increasingly more to do with the capital flow/'risk-on' tones which prevail through these periods," says strategist Richard Schellbach. Global investors appear to still be short the Australian equity market. But they "will be energized to continue building positions in Aussie stocks to take advantage of this currency-driven 'catch-up' trade," UBS says. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

1519 ET - Fed Chair Jerome Powell declines to respond to questions on his future as governor at the post-rate decision news conference. Powell's term as chair is set to expire in May but he has the option to remain on the Fed's board of policymakers until 2028. Among those in contention to become the new chair are National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett, former Fed governor Kevin Warsh, Fed governor Christopher Waller and Blackrock executive Rick Rieder. (jessica.coacci@wsj.com; jessica_coacci)

1514 ET - Fed Chair Jerome Powell says Lisa Cook's case is perhaps the most important legal case in the Fed's history when responding to a reporter's question of his attendance to the Supreme Court arguments on the Fed governor that took place last week. (jessica.coacci@wsj.com; jessica_coacci)

1512 ET - Treasury yields decline as Fed Chair Powell faces the press after the FOMC held interest rates steady as expected. Powell says policymakers see employment stabilizing while inflation remains above target. He adds that inflation expectations have declined. The assessment seems to set the stage for a prolonged pause, but Powell is typically noncommittal. He dodges questions about political pressure on the Fed to cut rates and about his succession later this year. The 10-year yield is at 4.245% and the two-year at 3.579%, both slightly above yesterday's levels. The WSJ Dollar Index also loses some steam during the Q&A and is now up 0.5%. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com)

1436 ET - Treasury yields change little and remain above yesterday's levels as the Fed leaves rates unchanged, as expected, and indicates its March decision will depend on upcoming data. The statement acknowledges robust GDP growth and stable unemployment rate, raising the question of how much weight the Fed will put on still elevated inflation, TD Wealth's Sid Vaidya says. The recent sequence of interest rate cuts supported employment. Vaidya suspects it could be an indication that "they are going to refocus on inflation." TD Wealth expects the Fed to cut twice this year, but not necessarily in its next meeting. The 10-year yield is at 4.269%. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1405 ET - There wasn't much suspense heading into the Fed's January meeting. One of the few open questions was how many governors would dissent against the Fed's widely expected decision to hold rates steady. Stephen Miran's dissent in favor of a cut was anticipated, but traders weren't sure whether governors Christopher Waller and Michelle Bowman would dissent as well. Waller joined Miran in dissent--a move that could help his case with President Trump as the president nears a final selection to succeed Chair Jerome Powell. Waller is the only internal candidate among the four finalists. Bowman chose to vote in line with Powell and the rest of the committee in favor of holding rates steady. (matt.grossman@wsj.com; @mattgrossman)

1405 ET - The Fed kept most of the logic of its policy statement intact this month, making only a few edits since December. But they signaled slightly more comfort with the state of the economy. In the first sentence of the statement, policymakers upgraded their assessment of economic growth to "solid," from the "moderate" label they applied in December. They also said that the unemployment rate "has shown some signs of stabilization," compared with in December, when they noted that unemployment had edged higher. A phrase about rising downside risks to unemployment was deleted. (matt.grossman@wsj.com; @mattgrossman)

1253 ET - The euro's strength against the dollar is an inevitable consequence of flows into European equities, UBS strategist Gerry Fowler says. Though a stronger euro will weigh on the earnings of companies that produce domestically and sell to international markets, like luxury goods and pharma companies, strong inflows to equities should support market performance, he says. Because of those flows, "to a certain extent the euro going up is going to be coincident with performance, if not driving performance," Fowler says. U.S. investors are looking to allocate to European equities on a scale not seen since before the financial crisis, he adds, though the resulting euro strengthening could hurt European companies' international competitiveness, he adds. (josephmichael.stonor@wsj.com)

1223 ET - Municipal bonds are a source of relative safety and solid income within an uneven economic environment, Touchstone's Crit Thomas says. He sees munis supported by generally healthy state and municipal finances, as local debt levels remain relatively low. Tax-exemptions bolster the appeal of munis to investors in the highest tax brackets. "I think that the tax equivalent yield that you can get from munis remains relatively attractive." Munis, he says, provide stable income and potential downside protection during periods of equity market volatility. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1221 ET - Low-risk bonds with intermediate maturities are the preferred option for fixed-income strategies, Touchstone's Crit Thomas says. He suggests focusing on the "belly of the curve," specifically maturities of five to seven years, while being cautious about longer durations due to persistent policy risks and a lack of fiscal discipline. "We're putting an emphasis on quality," he says. Thomas says investment-grade corporates are offering attractive yields and have shown resilience to macro and policy shocks. He maintains a more cautious, "underweight" stance on high-yield debt, arguing the compensation for taking on additional credit risk is low. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1155 ET - The difference between U.K. short-term and long-term gilt yields is expected to widen, Oxford Economics chief U.K. economist Andrew Goodwin says. The short-term yields are expected to fall as the Bank of England continues to cut interest rates, while long-term gilt yields are likely to face upward pressure due to fiscal concerns, he says at the Oxford Economics Global Economic Outlook Conference 2026 in London. The U.K. 2-year gilt yields last trade at 3.732%, Tradeweb data show. The U.K. 30-year gilt yields last trade at 5.293%. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

data.title: AT&T's earnings impress as subscriber growth booms and the stock jumps
data.description:
By Emily Bary

AT&T added 283,000 fiber subscribers and 421,000 net postpaid phone subscribers in the latest quarter, reflecting a push for 'convergence' of connectivity offerings

AT&T posted fourth-quarter earnings on Wednesday morning.

AT&T turned in another strong quarter of subscriber growth across its fiber and wireless business, reflecting its efforts to expand internet offerings and sell more bundled packages.

The company on Wednesday reported 283,000 fiber subscribers for the fourth quarter, ahead of the 265,000 FactSet consensus. AT&T (T) posted 421,000 postpaid phone net additions, a measure of those who pay for service after the billing period is complete, coming in a bit below the 425,000 FactSet consensus.

AT&T shared that it has a 42% "convergence" rate for its services, meaning more than four in 10 fiber subscribers also use the company's wireless network.

Revenue totaled $33.5 billion for the fourth quarter, edging out the $32.9 billion consensus view, while adjusted earnings per share of 52 cents cleared the 46-cent mark that analysts were modeling.

Shares rose 4.7% on Wednesday.

See also: Here's how Microsoft can prove the doubters wrong this earnings season

Mobility service revenue was up 2.4% from a year earlier, hitting $17 billion and matching the consensus view. AT&T generated $2.2 billion of fiber revenue, up 13.6%.

The company noted a 5.6% rise in operating expenses for the mobility segment, as greater unit sales meant higher expenses for things like equipment, advertising and bad debt.

Free cash flow, a closely watched metric for AT&T investors, came in at $4.2 billion, versus the $3.9 billion FactSet consensus. AT&T sports a 4.8% dividend yield, so investors look to free cash flow as a signal of how AT&T can honor its dividend commitment while buying back stock as part of a renewed emphasis on share repurchases. In comparison, the implied dividend for the S&P 500 index SPX is 1.14%, according to FactSet data.

The company is targeting more than $45 billion in capital returns from 2026 to 2028, including $8 billion of buybacks in 2026 and "a consistent pace of share repurchases through 2028."

Read: When Tesla reports earnings, this could be the biggest highlight

-Emily Bary

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Micron and other chip stocks are seeing a big boost after these comments
data.description:
By Britney Nguyen

Seagate says prices could go higher for its storage products as supply remains tight due to AI

Seagate's stock was up more than 15% on Wednesday.

Memory stocks were seeing a further boost on Wednesday, as upbeat commentary from two industry players suggested that booming demand for storage products could deliver financial benefits over an extended period.

Seagate (STX) reported earnings on Tuesday afternoon, with management saying that the price per terabyte of storage could either stay flat or rise due to artificial-intelligence-driven demand for storage solutions in data centers. That talk stood out to Evercore ISI analyst Amit Daryanani.

Meanwhile, Citi's Asiya Merchant flagged that Seagate has customers considering locking in 2028 agreements to secure access to a scarce commodity.

In Daryanani's view, Seagate is positioned to benefit from ongoing AI demand, as the tight supply-and-demand environment gives it more pricing power for its higher-capacity storage offerings. Daryanani is also optimistic about Seagate's leadership with its Heat-Assisted Magnetic Recording, or HAMR, technology, which allows it to make products with higher data density.

Seagate CEO Dave Mosley said in a statement that the company's strong December quarter reflected "the durability of data-center demand" and the ramp up of its HAMR-based products.

See more: Micron's stock is surging as the company looks to cash in on a 'desperate' market

Daryanani said a 22% increase in average nearline capacity per hard disk drive to 23 terabytes and a rise in capacity of Seagate's cloud nearline storage to 26 terabytes show the company's shift to offering more higher-capacity products, which also cost more for customers. Nearline storage is used for data that is not actively used, but that needs to be accessed quickly.

He also noted management's commentary on nearline capacity being fully allocated for the year, with visibility into demand for these products until 2028. That supports "a constructive supply-demand environment" that will drive both higher sales growth and better pricing, Daryanani said in a Tuesday note.

Seagate's stock was up more than 15% in Wednesday morning action. Western Digital's stock (WDC) was up nearly 8%, Sandisk's stock (SNDK) was up more than 7% and Micron Technology's stock (MU) was up about 4%.

"Just when you thought you were at max volume on the amplifier and could not turn it up any higher" the latest news and earnings in the semiconductor sector have made it so "more buyers come rushing in," Mizuho trading-desk analyst Jordan Klein said in a note to clients.

Cantor Fitzgerald analyst C.J. Muse said Seagate's pricing increases for its hard disk drives, along with higher prices for its HAMR products as well as cost reductions, are helping make the company more profitable. And Seagate's free cash flow "is only gaining momentum," Muse said in a Tuesday note.

Don't miss: Here's how Sandisk's stock can follow its 1,000% surge with even more gains

Additionally, South Korean memory-chip leader SK Hynix (KR:000660) said in its Wednesday morning earnings report that it is planning for a "considerable increase" in capital expenditures after seeing a 90% jump in profit and 66% increase in revenue in the fourth quarter. The company intends to boost its spending amid signals that it sees the rosy demand environment sustaining.

As AI workloads shift to inference, or the process of running AI models after training, memory and storage have become more crucial to data centers. Context windows, or the amount of data an AI model can process at one time, are getting longer, requiring more memory components to support a model's "working memory" for more complex responses.

SK Hynix is also a leader in high-bandwidth memory, alongside Samsung Electronics (KR:005930) and Micron, and the company emphasized its role in providing Nvidia (NVDA) with HBM3E for its Blackwell AI platform. The company also plans to support the upcoming Rubin platform with its next-generation HBM4.

Read also: ASML bookings blow past estimates as SK Hynix plans capital-expenditure boost

-Britney Nguyen

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.
